div.note: Subject: MORALE REPORT.
CONFIDENTIAL
Medical Section.
37 Beach Group. Ref.37/3.
4th March 1945.
O. C.
Headquarters.
Land Forces Adriatic.
Herewith quarterly report on the morale of this Section.
W.E. I Officer. 25 O.Rs.
Section I. Morale in general.
Morale in this Section is very good considering that it has never carried out the roll for which it was formed and trained.
There has been long periods of inactivity and doubt as to future roll, but when engaged in full time work of any kind personnel work together in excellent sprit and co-operation.
Section II. Factors affecting Morale. hhh I. Discipline.
Good which is shown by the absence of crime during the quarter. hhh II. Confidence.
Every confidence is held in all Leaders, Arms, Equipment and General efficiency. hhh III. Mail.
No complaints received regarding mail apart from irregularities of receipt of such during recent operations in Yugo slavia which were unavoidable. hhh IV. Welfare.
Living conditions excellent during all the quarter. Recreation and amusements were satisfactory in view of the difficulties of location during quarter. i.e. Yugo Slavia. hhh V. Education and ABCA.
Little attention has been given to the matter of Education and ABCA in this Section previously. This is having immediate attention. hhh VI. Relations between Officers and men.
Quite satisfactory. hhh VII. Relations with other Services.
Excellent. hhh VIII. Relations with Allies.

Attitude towards the Allies is towards friendliness and to avoid unpleasant incidents. The only exception that has come to my notice has been shown towards the Yugoslavia (Partisans) Which was brought about by the heavy restrictions imposed against fraternization. hhh IX. Home affairs.
To little is understood of these matters, due to:- (see para Five.) hhh X. Finance.
The personnel of this Section are clear of all debts. hhh XI. Miscellaneous.
Leave was very much overdue, but in February majority of the section had leave to Rome or local leave. By the middle of March all desiring leave will have had same.

LIAP. Two O.Rs are at present on leave in U.K. The first one was chosen by ballet, he had 2 yrs 1mth. The 2nd was selected from the ADMS L.F.A.pool , service 2 yrs5 mths. It is the opinion of the men following my talk to them, that 02E should do the selecting of personnel.
There is no doubt that whatever the method of sellecting there will always be a slight feeling of un­rest and dissatisfaction which I think is unavoidable
Relations with civilians. This feeling quite good but not encouraged.
(E.T.Shennan) Capt. R.A.M.C.
O.C. Medical Section 37 Beach Group. L.F.A.
DMG
